title: Finset union cardinality is not unconditionally additive
informal_statement: Finset union cardinality is not additive in general: there exist finite sets s, t of naturals with (s ∪ t).card ≠ s.card + t.card. The unconditional claim is false without a Disjoint s t hypothesis (the true statement is Finset.card_union_of_disjoint); witnessed here by s = t = {0}, where (s ∪ t).card = 1 but s.card + t.card = 2.
informal_proof_idea: Assume the universal claim for contradiction, instantiate at s = t = {0}, and let `simp` compute ({0} ∪ {0}).card = 1 and {0}.card + {0}.card = 2, closing the goal by the resulting numeral contradiction.
formal_statement_pp: theorem not_forall_card_union_eq_add_card : ¬ (∀ s t : Finset ℕ, (s ∪ t).card = s.card + t.card)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode f2bb04ed, problem_version b3a3381b-d332-4e59-af28-49d97a3524a6). Companion to the negative example negative.combinatorics.card_union_no_disjoint.false_generalization.v1, which captures the natural-but-false direct attempt on the unconditional (undisproved-negation) claim in a separate tracked episode (2e76ae52).
